title: verbascum × tschivrilense
intro: Verbascum × tschivrilense, also known as the hybrid Mullein, is a cross between Verbascum thapsus and Verbascum phoeniceum, two species of flowering plants in the figwort family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant qualities, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Verbascum × tschivrilense include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and promote relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Verbascum × tschivrilense can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used to treat a range of health issues, from minor skin irritations to more serious respiratory conditions.
